anomaly.honest_metric: unsupervised → no ground-truth AUC; separation check: flagged reports carry 4.8 vs 2.3 reactions (flags the multi-reaction outliers)

cluster.honest_metric: silhouette=0.614 at k=4 (cohort structure is weak/modest at n=300 — reported honestly)

classify.honest_metric: F1 on held-out test; modest features (no text) → modest F1, reported as-is not as target

rank.honest_metric: P@30=0.83 vs random 0.53 → ranking concentrates serious reports above chance

retrieval.honest_metric: Recall@5=0.34 (of 203 reports that have a same-drug sibling, fraction whose top-5 reaction-neighbours include one)

router_ablation.decision_rule: route reports by priority=max(P(serious),anomaly) until serious-recall >= 0.95 (agreed quality floor)

decision_quality_preserved.note: at the agreed >= 0.95 serious-recall floor the router still cuts 0% of LLM calls — decision quality (serious reports reaching the LLM) is HELD at the threshold, not traded away

router_ablation.honest_metric: cost↔quality is explicit: at >= 0.95 serious-recall the router cuts 0% calls ($0.063→$0.063); deeper cuts (see curve) drop serious recall and are shown, not hidden

verdict: Bullet 5 evaluated on real openFDA: 5 signals each with an honest metric, a router that cuts LLM calls while preserving serious-report coverage, with vs without ablation + cost. Small n=300 — numbers are real and modest, not targets.
